tagged_story_for_tts: <[silence:3400ms]>First letter. <[silence:1300ms]> Alphabet’s anchor.<[silence:1300ms]>"A" comes from the Phoenician aleph—a symbol for "ox," shaped like its head, horns and all.<[silence:700ms]>The Greeks flipped it to alpha, keeping the sound but dropping the bovine meaning.<[silence:1300ms]>It’s the first because early scribes listed letters by shape—aleph’s simple strokes made it easiest to carve into clay or stone.<[silence:700ms]>Like a key turning a lock, "A" unlocks every alphabet that followed: Latin, Cyrillic, even Korean’s ㄱ.<[silence:1300ms]>In music, "A4" vibrates at 440 Hz—a tuning standard since 1939, when musicians agreed to end the chaos of mismatched pitches.<[silence:700ms]>In math, A labels variables, matrices, even Einstein’s cosmological constant.<[silence:700ms]>Yet its simplest power?<[silence:700ms]>A child’s first scrawl—two slanting lines, crossed by one—marks the start of literacy.<[silence:2000ms]>Three related subjects are symbol evolution, phonetic shifts, and Braille dots.<[silence:1200ms]>
clean_story: First letter.  Alphabet’s anchor. "A" comes from the Phoenician aleph—a symbol for "ox," shaped like its head, horns and all. The Greeks flipped it to alpha, keeping the sound but dropping the bovine meaning. It’s the first because early scribes listed letters by shape—aleph’s simple strokes made it easiest to carve into clay or stone. Like a key turning a lock, "A" unlocks every alphabet that followed: Latin, Cyrillic, even Korean’s ㄱ. In music, "A4" vibrates at 440 Hz—a tuning standard since 1939, when musicians agreed to end the chaos of mismatched pitches. In math, A labels variables, matrices, even Einstein’s cosmological constant. Yet its simplest power?A child’s first scrawl—two slanting lines, crossed by one—marks the start of literacy. Three related subjects are symbol evolution, phonetic shifts, and Braille dots.
